steps.title: Use the standard circle equation
steps.content: A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation$$(x-h)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=r^{2}.$$Here $(h,k)=(-8,6)$ and $r=5$, so $r^{2}=25$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $(x-8)^{2}+(y+6)^{2}=25$: center $(8,-6)$, radius 5 — wrong center.
  • B) $(x+8)^{2}+(y-6)^{2}=25$: center $(-8,6)$, radius 5 — matches.
  • C) $(x-8)^{2}+(y+6)^{2}=5$: wrong center and radius $\sqrt{5}$.
  • D) $(x+8)^{2}+(y-6)^{2}=5$: correct center but radius $\sqrt{5}$, not 5.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
